candidates.name: Kay Ivey
candidates.notes: Longest-serving current female governor in the US. Signed the Human Life Protection Act (2019) — nation's most restrictive abortion ban with no exceptions for rape or incest, making it a Class A felony for doctors. Signed constitutional carry (2023). Signed anti-DEI legislation for public universities. Allowed religious adoption agencies to refuse LGBT couples. Signed monument protection law (Confederate monuments). Signed anti-CRT legislation. Endorsed Trump. Final term — cannot run again in 2026 due to term limits.

profile.background: Former high school teacher and bank officer; served as state treasurer and lieutenant governor before becoming Alabama's 54th governor in 2017.

candidates.name: Tommy Tuberville
candidates.notes: Former college football coach turned senator. Blocked 300+ military promotions for 10 months over Pentagon abortion travel policy — praised by pro-life groups, criticized by Senate GOP leadership. One of 8 senators who voted not to certify 2020 election results. Heritage Action 98% in 118th Congress. Self-described 'man of Christian faith.' Opposes all gun control. Supports school choice, parental rights, opposes CRT. Strong Trump ally. Running for Alabama Governor in 2026 — announced he won't seek Senate re-election.

profile.background: Former major college football head coach at Ole Miss, Auburn, Texas Tech, and Cincinnati. Led Auburn to undefeated 2004 season. ESPN analyst. No prior political experience before Senate.
profile.office: 455 Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 41 West I-65, Service Rd. N., Suite 2300-A, 3BB&T Centre
One Church St., Suite 500-B, Frank M Johnson Jr. Annex

candidates.name: Katie Britt
candidates.notes: First woman elected to US Senate from Alabama. Youngest Republican woman senator. Gained national attention for emotional GOP response to Biden's 2024 State of the Union — widely mocked but energized conservative base. Perfect 100% NumbersUSA immigration score. Pro-life, pro-2A, supports school choice. Led Kids Off Social Media Act (KOSMA) with bipartisan support. Former Chief of Staff to Sen. Shelby and CEO of Business Council of Alabama. Husband Wesley Britt played in NFL. Heritage Action 73% — more moderate than Tuberville's 98% but still solidly conservative. Supports Israel as 'Christian conservative.'

profile.education: University of Alabama (BA Political Science), University of Alabama School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former chief of staff to Senator Richard Shelby and first female CEO of Business Council of Alabama. Youngest Republican woman ever elected to U.S. Senate. Practiced law at Butler Snow.
profile.office: 416 Russell Senate Office Building
